body.title: [in_progress] harvest_run.py operator runner + first live harvest run on Hex
body.description:
**Problem:** the harvest machine is complete + proven (escalation-off + stuck-ladder + env toggle + cwd isolation) but has never RUN. Akien 2026-07-05: wire toggle + do the first harvest run to GENERATE the corpus the classifier/resume tickets need.

**Proposed shape:** a small reusable operator script devlab/claudecode/harvest_run.py that (a) creates an isolated scratch workdir (NOT the live repo — cwd threaded via T-ds-domain-cwd-isolation), (b) sets INFERENCE_ENDPOINT=hex.local + UU_HARVEST_MODE=1, (c) runs resolve_domain('coding').run(ticket, cwd=scratch) on a synthetic throwaway ticket chosen to reach a capability wall on devstral-24b, (d) reports the LoopResult + the emitted rung-choice record(s) + the io_corpus entries (asserting provider=ollama, model=devstral/qwen — NOT openrouter, else the run is a routing artifact not a capability wall). Writes to the REAL stores (that is the point — real harvest data).

**Affected files:** devlab/claudecode/harvest_run.py (new).

**Design rules:** cwd MUST be an isolated dir (CP6 — edit-capable model off the live tree); confirm route is Hex ollama free before trusting the wall; no SQLite; log the run.

**Scope boundary:** the runner + one first run. NOT the classifier, NOT a scheduled/repeated harvest loop.

**Validation (observation, not red-green — advisor):** the first run produces >=1 io_corpus record with provider=ollama (Hex, $0) AND >=1 rung-choice record in inference_starve for the run's ticket. That pair IS the proof the harvest aperture is open end-to-end.

**Observable via:** harvest_run.py prints the corpus provider/model + the rung record; the inference_starve/*.rungchoice.jsonl file gains a row.

**Test plan:** no red-green harness — this is a live observation run (close shipped-unproven if the run cannot complete, naming the blocker; close proven-by-observation with the corpus+rung evidence if it does). audit-emphasis: doc-only-ish (operator script).

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
